skill.name: twitter-automation
skill.description: Guides authenticated Twitter/X automation through Rube MCP for posts, search, users, bookmarks, lists, and media workflows.
skill.summary: Use Rube MCP to perform authenticated Twitter/X posting, search, lookup, bookmark, list, and media workflows.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, crypto, and reconnaissance patterns, but review found these are Markdown tool names, parameters, and documentation text rather than executable code. The skill still carries medium risk because it directs authenticated Twitter/X actions through a remote Rube MCP endpoint, including posting, deletion, media upload, bookmarks, and account-specific lookups. No prompt injection attempt or confirmed malicious intent was found.

medium_findings.title: Authenticated Twitter/X Actions Through Remote MCP
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs users to add a remote Rube MCP endpoint and use authenticated Twitter tools. This can create, delete, upload media for, bookmark, and unlike posts, so misuse or overbroad authorization can affect a real social account. The behavior is expected for this skill and is not malicious, but it requires user review and confirmation before account-changing actions.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documented workflows explicitly use a remote MCP service and authenticated Twitter tool calls. The intent is legitimate automation, but the account-changing capability is clear.

medium_findings.title: Non-Idempotent Posting Workflow
medium_findings.description: The skill warns that post creation is not idempotent and retries can create duplicate posts. Automated posting workflows should require deduplication and explicit confirmation to prevent accidental repeated publication.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill directly documents duplicate-post risk and gives mitigation guidance. This is an operational security and safety concern, not evidence of malware.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution Findings Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The many Ruby or shell backtick findings point to Markdown inline code spans naming Rube and Twitter tools, parameters, and examples. No executable Ruby, shell script, or command invocation was found in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are documentation and Markdown code spans only. There is no source file or syntax that would execute shell commands.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are Documentation False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm alerts do not correspond to cryptographic code. The cited lines are the skill description and a Twitter user fields parameter description.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations contain descriptive text, not hashing, encryption, or signature logic. No weak crypto implementation is present in the reviewed file.

low_findings.title: Reconnaissance Findings Are API Documentation False Positives
low_findings.description: The system and network reconnaissance alerts point to Twitter lookup tools, list endpoints, access tier notes, and quick reference rows. These describe Twitter API workflows, not host or network scanning behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are table entries and notes for Twitter/X API actions. They do not instruct local host discovery, port scanning, or network reconnaissance.

dangerous_patterns.title: Remote Service Dependency
dangerous_patterns.description: The setup instructs users to add https://rube.app/mcp as an MCP server. This is required for the skill but should be treated as third-party network access.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The remote endpoint is explicitly documented. This is a legitimate dependency, but it expands the trust boundary.

dangerous_patterns.title: Account-Changing Social Media Operations
dangerous_patterns.description: The documented tool sequences include creating posts, deleting posts, uploading media, adding bookmarks, removing bookmarks, and unliking posts. These operations can modify the authenticated account state.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The tool names and workflow descriptions clearly identify state-changing Twitter/X actions. No hidden behavior was found beyond the documented purpose.

content.value_statement: Managing Twitter/X actions through an AI assistant can be slow and error-prone. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code structured Rube MCP workflows for posts, search, users, bookmarks, lists, and media.
actual_capabilities: Checks Rube MCP availability and guides Twitter OAuth connection setup.
Creates, looks up, deletes, and replies to Twitter/X posts.
Uploads image, video, or GIF media before creating a post.
Searches recent posts, archive posts, and search result counts.
Looks up users by authenticated account, username, user ID, or ID batch.
Lists and manages bookmarks, lists, liked posts, and unlikes.
limitations: Requires Rube MCP and an active Twitter/X OAuth connection.
Twitter/X rate limits and access tiers can block some workflows.
Post creation is not idempotent and can create duplicates on retry.
Tool schemas can change, so the skill requires searching tools first.

use_cases.title: Publish Approved Updates
use_cases.description: Prepare and publish account updates after confirming text, media, and target account details.

use_cases.title: Monitor Recent Conversations
use_cases.description: Search recent Twitter/X posts with operators and summarize matching conversations for review.

use_cases.title: Organize Saved Content
use_cases.description: List bookmarks, manage saved posts, and inspect account lists through authenticated workflows.

prompt_templates.title: Check Connection
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the twitter-automation skill to verify Rube MCP is available and confirm whether my Twitter connection is active.

prompt_templates.title: Search Recent Posts
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the twitter-automation skill to search recent Twitter/X posts about [topic]. Return a concise summary and note any rate limit issues.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a Post With Media
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the twitter-automation skill to prepare a Twitter/X post with this text and attached media. Show me the final post for approval before publishing.

prompt_templates.title: Run an Account Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the twitter-automation skill to review my recent bookmarks, owned lists, and liked posts. Summarize useful items and ask before making changes.

output_examples.input: Check whether my Twitter/X connection is ready.
output_examples.output: The assistant confirms that Rube MCP responds, checks the Twitter toolkit connection, and reports whether OAuth is active.

output_examples.input: Find recent posts from a specific username about a launch.
output_examples.output: The assistant builds the search query, retrieves recent matches, and summarizes the main posts with dates and authors.

output_examples.input: Post this update with an image after I approve it.
output_examples.output: The assistant uploads the image, previews the final post text, requests approval, and only then creates the post.

best_practices: Search for current Rube tool schemas before each workflow.
Require explicit user confirmation before posting, deleting, or changing bookmarks.
Track post attempts to avoid duplicate posts after timeouts.
anti_patterns: Do not retry post creation automatically after an uncertain timeout.
Do not publish or delete content without showing the final action first.
Do not assume archive search is available without checking the account access tier.

faq.question: Does this skill require Twitter/X login?
faq.answer: Yes. It requires an active Twitter/X connection through Rube MCP and OAuth.

faq.question: Can it publish posts automatically?
faq.answer: It can guide post creation, but users should require approval before publishing.

faq.question: Can it upload media?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents image uploads and large media uploads for videos or GIFs.

faq.question: Can it search old posts?
faq.answer: It documents full archive search, but that may require Academic Research or Enterprise access.

faq.question: Does it manage bookmarks?
faq.answer: Yes. It can list, add, and remove bookmarks for the authenticated user.

faq.question: What is the main safety concern?
faq.answer: The main concern is account-changing actions through an authenticated remote service.
